title: PAIK Common Frontmatter
description: Fields shared by every PAIK document, plus the reusable owner/link building blocks every document draws on. Aligned with the Open Knowledge Format (OKF) v0.1 base frontmatter (type/title/description) - see SPEC.md section 2 and the 'Relationship to Open Knowledge Format' section.

owner.description: Who is responsible for this document's subject. Inline by design - PAIK has no separate team/roster document type; the org's own directory (HR system, chat admin, Backstage) stays the source of truth for who is actually on a team.

name.description: Team name, e.g. "Orders team" - never an individual's name; PAIK does not identify individual people anywhere in a document

ref.description: Optional link to where this owner is documented in the org's own systems (a wiki page, an org chart) - never a list of personal emails/handles here

link.description: A single typed pointer to an external system or resource. `kind` is the only enforced key, on purpose - one shape covers ticketing, wikis, API specs, repos, external services, secrets locations, dashboards, runbooks, on-call, and anything else. See SPEC.md section 4 for common `kind` values. Fields beyond `kind` are intentionally open (unlike the rest of a PAIK document), since the whole point of `links` is not needing a new schema per system. This `kind` is unrelated to a document's own top-level `type` (OKF concept type) - it's a nested, PAIK-only field.

kind.description: What this link points at, e.g. repository, api, jira-project, jira-epic, jira-component, confluence, external-service, secrets, dashboard, runbook, pagerduty, status-page, deploy-pipeline, chat, other

id.description: The identifier this link's system uses internally, e.g. a Jira project key or component key

purpose.description: Free-text disambiguator when a kind can appear more than once on the same document, e.g. purpose: architecture on a second confluence link

provider.description: The vendor/product behind this link, e.g. jira, confluence, github, gitlab, swaggerhub, pagerduty, vault - never a protocol or file format (those are protocol/format)

protocol.description: The wire protocol for an api link, e.g. grpc, graphql, rest, soap

format.description: The spec/document format for an api link, e.g. openapi, asyncapi

component.description: When this document is shared across more than one component (typically an environment.md deployed by several services), the id of the single component this link actually applies to. Omit when the link applies to the whole document.

environment.description: When this document is shared across more than one environment (typically a component.md deployed to several environments), the id of the single environment this link actually applies to. Omit when the link applies to every environment the document lists.

type.description: OKF concept type - the one field the Open Knowledge Format itself requires, non-empty, on every concept document. Each PAIK document kind gives this a fixed value: paik-project, paik-component, or paik-environment (enforced by that kind's own schema, not here).

description.description: OKF-recommended single-sentence summary. Optional at the common level; individual kinds may require or refine it (project.md requires a richer one - see project.schema.json).

paik.description: PAIK spec version this document conforms to

id.description: Stable kebab-case slug, unique within the project. Distinct from OKF's own concept identity, which is always the document's file path with the .md suffix removed - the two commonly agree (a file named orders-api.md with id: orders-api) but are not the same mechanism, and PAIK's id is what the rest of this spec (depends_on, component/environment qualifiers, ...) actually resolves against.

extensions.description: Free-form, namespaced fields for org-specific needs that don't belong in the core schema, e.g. "company.hu/data-owner": "finance". Namespace keys as domain/key to avoid collisions; this is intentionally not validated further.
